assertions.title: Canonical entry points agree on current truth
assertions.reason: The primary entry points consistently route readers into docs/ as the public truth surface, and .factory/library is called out only as internal worker guidance rather than active reader-facing documentation.
evidence: README.md — 'Documentation Spine' points to docs/INDEX.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/frontend-portals/README.md, docs/frontend-api/README.md, and docs/deprecated/INDEX.md; README also states '.factory/library/*' is internal worker guidance.
ARCHITECTURE.md — repo-root file self-describes as a signpost into the canonical docs spine and routes to docs/INDEX.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, and docs/deprecated/INDEX.md.
docs/INDEX.md — states it is the canonical entrypoint and says public runtime/deployment truth lives under docs/ while '.factory/library/*' is internal guidance.
docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — self-describes as the canonical runtime architecture reference and points back to docs/INDEX.md and repo-root ARCHITECTURE.md as the signpost.

assertions.title: Current docs are reachable from the canonical spine
assertions.reason: docs/INDEX.md says packets not reachable through the index are not part of the canonical docs tree, but several retained docs outside that spine remain unlabeled and look current instead of being clearly marked as non-canonical/reference-only.
evidence: docs/INDEX.md — declares that every major docs section is linked from the index and that packets not reachable through it are not part of the canonical docs tree.
docs/migration-guide.md — present under docs/ with no non-canonical/reference banner and not listed in docs/INDEX.md.
docs/ACCOUNTING_PORTAL_SCOPE_GUARDRAIL.md — present under docs/ with no non-canonical/reference banner and not listed in docs/INDEX.md.
docs/AUDIT_TRAIL_OWNERSHIP.md — present under docs/ with no non-canonical/reference banner and not listed in docs/INDEX.md.
docs/accounting-portal-endpoint-map.md — present under docs/ with no non-canonical/reference banner and not listed in docs/INDEX.md.
docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — links to docs/developer/onboarding-stock-readiness/06-stock-truth-decision.md, but docs/INDEX.md does not classify that developer packet.

assertions.title: Superseded docs are deleted or explicitly retired
assertions.reason: At least one retained stale surface is not explicitly retired: docs/workflows/data-migration.md warns that docs/migration-guide.md may be outdated and points readers to docs/runbooks/migrations.md, but docs/migration-guide.md itself has no retirement banner and is absent from the retirement registry.
evidence: docs/CONVENTIONS.md — stale-doc policy requires legacy docs to be redirected, archived, or marked non-canonical/reference-only.
docs/workflows/data-migration.md — marked NON-CANONICAL and says docs/migration-guide.md 'may be outdated' while directing readers to docs/runbooks/migrations.md.
docs/migration-guide.md — still presents itself as a normal guide with no non-canonical/reference-only banner.
docs/deprecated/INDEX.md — contains no entry for docs/migration-guide.md.

assertions.title: Retirement registry covers hard cuts
assertions.reason: The retirement registry does not cover all retained hard-cut docs surfaces. It lists developer-guide and endpoint-inventory, but not the legacy frontend handoff docs or the historical workflow guides that the canonical index and flow inventory still surface as reference material.
evidence: docs/deprecated/INDEX.md — 'Historical Documentation' only registers docs/developer-guide.md and docs/endpoint-inventory.md.

docs/INDEX.md — still lists frontend-handoff-platform.md, frontend-handoff-operations.md, frontend-handoff-commercial.md, frontend-handoff-finance.md, and accounting-portal-frontend-engineer-handoff.md as retained reference-only docs.

docs/flows/FLOW-INVENTORY.md — still lists docs/workflows/admin-and-tenant-management.md, manufacturing-and-packaging.md, inventory-management.md, sales-order-to-cash.md, purchase-to-pay.md, accounting-and-period-close.md, payroll.md, and data-migration.md as historical workflow guides.
docs/frontend-handoff-platform.md and docs/workflows/payroll.md — retained legacy docs exist, but docs/deprecated/INDEX.md does not give readers a registry entry with their replacement path.

assertions.title: Canonical naming matches the approved product identity
assertions.reason: The canonical docs spine uses mixed product identity. The entrypoints use orchestrator-erp, but multiple canonical docs still describe the current product as BigBright ERP.
evidence: README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/INDEX.md, and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — use orchestrator-erp naming on their entrypoint titles/introductions.
docs/CONVENTIONS.md — still says 'BigBright ERP backend documentation library'.
docs/BACKEND-FEATURE-CATALOG.md — still says 'BigBright ERP backend feature landscape'.
docs/RECOMMENDATIONS.md — still says it is the canonical recommendations surface for the 'BigBright ERP backend'.
docs/RELIABILITY.md and docs/flows/FLOW-INVENTORY.md — still title/describe the product as BigBright ERP.

assertions.title: Index and file-local canonical status agree
assertions.reason: The sampled files inspected for index/registry status agree with their file-local banners or self-descriptions: canonical files describe themselves as canonical, while retained reference docs label themselves as non-canonical/reference-only in line with the index and retirement registry.
evidence: docs/INDEX.md + docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — index presents docs/ARCHITECTURE.md as canonical runtime architecture, and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md self-describes as the canonical runtime architecture reference.
docs/INDEX.md + docs/deprecated/INDEX.md + docs/developer-guide.md — indexes call developer-guide non-canonical/superseded, and the file banner says NON-CANONICAL.
docs/INDEX.md + docs/deprecated/INDEX.md + docs/endpoint-inventory.md — indexes call endpoint-inventory reference-only, and the file banner says REFERENCE ONLY.
docs/INDEX.md + docs/frontend-handoff-platform.md — index marks legacy frontend handoffs reference-only, and the file banner says NON-CANONICAL / REFERENCE ONLY.

frictions.description: A broad grep over docs classification/naming text was truncated because the repo contains many markdown matches.
frictions.resolution: Narrowed the search to specific canonical and retained doc surfaces, then confirmed results with direct file reads.

summary: Tested 6 docs-spine assertions via read-only repo inspection: 2 passed (VAL-DOCS-001, VAL-DOCS-008) and 4 failed (VAL-DOCS-002, VAL-DOCS-003, VAL-DOCS-004, VAL-DOCS-007).
